relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks diagnostic care to Moses and Aaron.
Skin changes are brought for priestly inspection, not moral accusation.
Depth and hair change determine ritual state.
Unclear cases wait seven days.
If unchanged, waiting continues.
If faded and not spread, washing returns the person clean.
If it spreads after cleansing, it is shown again.
Spread marks uncleanness.
A scale affliction is brought to the priest.
White swelling and raw flesh are examined.
Chronic affliction is named without further isolation test.
Full covering is examined head to foot.
If all has turned white, the person is clean.
Raw flesh changes the state.
Raw flesh is ritually unclean.
If raw flesh turns white, the person returns to priest.
White transformation restores clean status.
A healed boil may leave a mark.
The mark is shown to the priest.
Depth and white hair signal affliction.
If not deep and faded, waiting is required.
Spread changes the judgment.
A stable scar is clean.
A burn mark may need inspection.
Depth and white hair mark scale affliction.
Faded surface marks wait seven days.
Spread brings uncleanness.
A faded burn scar is clean.
Head or beard affliction is inspected.
Depth and yellow hair mark scall.
Unclear scall is shut up seven days.
No spread means continued careful inspection.
The area is shaved except the affliction.
No spread means washing and clean status.
Spread after cleansing is examined.
Spread alone is enough to declare uncleanness.
Black hair and stability show healing.
White spots can appear on skin.
Dull white rash is clean.
Ordinary baldness is clean.
Forehead baldness is clean.
Affliction on bald skin is inspected.
Reddish-white swelling is examined.
The priest declares the ritual state.
The afflicted person signals ritual uncleanness publicly.
Outside-camp dwelling marks ritual separation, not loss of worth.
Even garments can bear affliction.
Wool, linen, skin, warp, and woof are inspected.
Greenish or reddish affliction is shown to priest.
The affected thing is shut up seven days.
Spread marks malignant affliction.
Severely afflicted material is burned.
If it has not spread, washing is commanded.
Washed material waits again.
Unchanged affliction is burned.
Faded affliction may be torn out.
Returning affliction is burned.
If the mark turns aside, washing restores clean status.
This is instruction for declaring clean or unclean.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 13 is careful ritual diagnosis, not stigma: the priest looks, waits, rechecks, washes, and often declares clean. Human worth is never the question.
